_notes.purpose: Second domestic profile (Iter-7). Synthetic US ecommerce / consumer-retail loan scenario. Numerics are ILLUSTRATIVE and pending live-source binding in a later iteration when snapshot pipelines mature. Inherits skeleton + stress_timing from _base_domestic.json via deep merge.
_notes.component_choices: Components match design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md §Component-to-audience-matrix 'Ecommerce' column: base_sofr (always), demand_volatility (core), inventory_carrying (core), wc (yes), payment_cycle (yes). NOTE: the matrix keeps payment_cycle as 'yes' for ecommerce — inventory_carrying is ADDED to the domestic skeleton, it does NOT replace payment_cycle. This corrects the _base_domestic.json _notes annotation ('substitutes inventory_carrying for payment_cycle'), which read the matrix as a removal; the catalog shows ecommerce keeps both. Post-merge component order is [base_sofr, demand_volatility, payment_cycle, wc, inventory_carrying]: the deep-merge preserves _base_domestic's base order for the 4 shared components and appends inventory_carrying (overlay-only). The composer's _build_sofr_path sums fractions additively, so this order is numerically gauge-safe; it is kept stable by this file.
_notes.no_base_change: Iter-7 deliberately does NOT add a _base_ecommerce.json (two _base files in domestic/ both with applies_to={mode:domestic} would both classify as the 'base' layer in profile_resolver._scan_candidates -> duplicate-layer error) and does NOT refactor _base_domestic.json (us-services depends on its skeleton). Ecommerce being a superset of the domestic skeleton + inventory_carrying makes both unnecessary. No profile_resolver.py change required.
_notes.industry_choice: applies_to.industry='ecommerce' is the schema-permitted slot (risk-factor-profile.schema.json applies_to.industry is type:string minLength:1). State.business_identity.mode='domestic_ecommerce' is matched to this profile by profile_resolver's domestic branch (_classify: applies_to.mode=='domestic' + industry=='ecommerce').
_notes.source_strategy: All source.type='config_file' per Iter-6 Flag-2 resolution (LEGAL_SOURCE_TYPES unchanged — 'snapshot' is NOT a 4th type). source.ref points to fixture paths under backend/tests/fixtures/snapshots/; snapshot_date stamps the reference date. Live API binding scheduled for a later iteration.
_notes.synthetic_values_warning: PMI / DSO / ISR / sensitivity values below are ILLUSTRATIVE for the Iter-7 composer test. They are NOT sourced from current ISM, Census MRTS, FRED ISRATIO, Atradius, or Intuit releases. base_sofr FED_PATH numbers (4.50/5.50/4.75) DO match the V1 anchor — the US base rate is shared with US-importer export-import scenarios. The inventory_carrying closed form is SIGNED OFF as the Iter-7 v1 baseline (see backend/components/inventory_carrying.py docstring); a fuller CCC-style (DIO+DSO−DPO) form is deferred to a future iteration without architecture change. Its 9-point contribution remains illustrative only because these INPUTS are synthetic, not because the form is unsettled.

source.citation: V1 inline FED_PATH (initial 4.50%, peak 5.50%, final 4.75%); the US base rate applies identically to US-domestic and US-importer scenarios. Live binding: FRED SOFR + Fed dot-plot in a later iteration.

Synthetic Iter-7 fixture (pmi_current=47.0 indicates moderate contraction in consumer-facing demand; pmi_neutral=50.0 PMI convention). Live binding: ISM/S&P Global PMI + Census E-commerce Retail in a later iteration. Formula spec per design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md §b.1.

Synthetic Iter-7 fixture; observed DSO 45d vs sector median 35d reflects marketplace/B2B ecommerce receivables running longer than card-settled DTC. Live binding: Atradius PPB + Intuit QuickBooks SMB Late Payments in a later iteration. Formula spec per design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md §b.5. Matrix keeps payment_cycle as 'yes' for ecommerce.

V1 inline WC base (30bps) carried forward; peak bumped to 60bps (illustrative) to reflect higher inventory-financing intensity of ecommerce vs services. Live binding: FRED ISRATIO + sector inventory-to-sales in a later iteration.

Synthetic Iter-7 fixture; ISR observed 1.62 vs historic mean 1.45 indicates a mild glut (catalog §b.2: >1.6 = gluts). dio_sensitivity=1.0 placeholder. Live binding: FRED ISRATIO + Census M3/MRTS + ISM Inventories in a later iteration. CORE ecommerce component per design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md §Component-to-audience-matrix. Closed form SIGNED OFF as Iter-7 v1 baseline; CCC-style (DIO+DSO−DPO) deferred (see backend/components/inventory_carrying.py).
